title: Substance use screening documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

investigations.text: Supports documenting why alcohol use was reviewed and the primary-care screening context without asserting hazardous use.
investigations.rationale: Supports alcohol screening in primary-care context for substance-use screening; no decision is generated.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports documenting why alcohol use was reviewed and the primary-care screening context without asserting hazardous use.

source.title: AUDIT: The Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test — Guidelines for Use in Primary Care, Second Edition

exact_section.heading: Purpose of the manual and context of alcohol screening

investigations.text: Supports recording alcohol frequency, typical quantity, heavy-use episodes, dependence-related symptoms, harmful consequences, concern from others, and prior injury without automatically scoring or diagnosing.
investigations.rationale: Supports AUDIT alcohol consumption and harm domains for substance-use screening; no decision is generated.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports recording alcohol frequency, typical quantity, heavy-use episodes, dependence-related symptoms, harmful consequences, concern from others, and prior injury without automatically scoring or diagnosing.

source.title: AUDIT: The Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test — Guidelines for Use in Primary Care, Second Edition

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Substance, route, amount, frequency, duration, last use, intoxication, withdrawal, harms, dependence, mental state, risk, diagnosis and plan remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for substance-use screening.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
